title: What is an FIR?
oneLineMeaning: First Information Report is the initial document that registers a crime with the police.
whyItMatters: Filing an FIR sets the criminal justice process in motion. Without it, most criminal cases cannot proceed.
coreRule: Under CrPC Section 154, any person can report a cognizable offense to the police, who must register it and investigate.
example: If you witness a theft, you file an FIR at the police station, leading to investigation.
takeaway: An FIR is the first step. Delay or refusal can be challenged in court.

title: What is Anticipatory Bail?
oneLineMeaning: Advance bail granted before arrest to protect against imminent custody.
whyItMatters: Allows individuals to avoid pre-trial detention when they fear false accusations.
coreRule: Section 438 of CrPC enables courts to grant anticipatory bail.
example: A businessperson anticipating false fraud charges can seek anticipatory bail.
takeaway: Protects against harassment while ensuring cooperation.

title: What is Arbitration?
oneLineMeaning: Private dispute resolution by a neutral third party instead of court.
whyItMatters: Faster, confidential, and cheaper than traditional litigation.
coreRule: Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 enforces arbitration awards.
example: Contract dispute resolved by an arbitrator whose decision is binding.

title: What is Money Laundering?
oneLineMeaning: Processing illegal money to appear legitimate through hidden transactions.
whyItMatters: A serious crime that finances terrorism and organized crime.
coreRule: Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002 criminalizes proceeds of crime.
example: Breaking large cash deposits into smaller amounts to avoid reporting.
takeaway: Compliance and KYC are essential defenses.

title: What is Consideration in Contracts?
oneLineMeaning: Something of value exchanged between parties to form a valid contract.
whyItMatters: Without consideration, a contract is generally unenforceable.
coreRule: Indian Contract Act, Section 2(d) defines consideration.
example: You pay ₹500 for a book - payment is consideration.
takeaway: Always ensure consideration is clearly stated.

title: What is Significant Influence?
oneLineMeaning: Power to affect company decisions without formal control.
coreRule: AS 21 defines significant influence as 20%+ voting power.

title: What is Oppression and Mismanagement?
oneLineMeaning: Minority shareholder protection against majority abuse.
whyItMatters: Provides legal remedy when shareholders treated unfairly.
coreRule: Section 397-398 of Companies Act allows NCLT petition.
example: Minority shareholders ousted from management can petition NCLT.

title: What is Insider Trading?
oneLineMeaning: Trading securities using unpublished price-sensitive information.
whyItMatters: Serious offense that undermines market integrity.
coreRule: SEBI Insider Trading Regulations, 2015 prohibit UPSI trading.
example: CEO selling shares before announcing poor earnings.

title: What is a Writ Petition?
oneLineMeaning: Direct constitutional remedy against state action violating rights.
whyItMatters: Fastest way to challenge government violations.
example: Filing habeas corpus if wrongly detained.

title: What is Corporate Veil?
oneLineMeaning: Legal separation between a company and its owners.
coreRule: Courts can pierce veil under fraud or evasion.
example: Using company to evade personal debts.

title: What is Specific Performance?
whyItMatters: Unique goods cannot be substituted with damages.
coreRule: Specific Relief Act enables specific performance.
example: Court ordering sale of heritage property.

title: What is Mens Rea?
oneLineMeaning: Guilty mind - criminal intent or knowledge of wrongdoing.
coreRule: Requires actus reus AND mens rea.

title: What is Res Judicata?
oneLineMeaning: Same matter cannot be litigated twice.
coreRule: CPC Section 11 bars same cause action.
example: Suing and losing bars same suit.

title: What is Cheque Bounce?
oneLineMeaning: Dishonor of cheque due to insufficient funds.
whyItMatters: Criminal offense under Section 138 of NI Act.
coreRule: Cheque bounce is criminal within validity period.
example: Cheque bounces due to stop payment.

title: What is Copyright Infringement?
oneLineMeaning: Unauthorized use of protected creative works.

title: What is Limited Liability?
oneLineMeaning: Shareholder liability limited to unpaid capital.
whyItMatters: Protects personal assets from business debts.
coreRule: LL companies shield owners from personal liability.
example: Company debts are not personal debts.

title: What is a Show Cause Notice?
oneLineMeaning: Formal notice requiring explanation before action.
coreRule: Natural justice requires opportunity to explain.
